_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for the endocrine glands whole-body map (Class 11 Chemical Coordination and Integration). Image keyed via prepare_illustration.py's new 'white' background mode (added 2026-07-15) -- the source PNG had an opaque white background despite the prompt asking for solid deep-charcoal #121316 (visible live in ChatGPT's UI as dark, but the downloaded raster was flattened onto white). Hotspots authored by visual inspection of the cropped/keyed webp, NOT yet browser-verified live -- recommend a quick pass in the prototype to confirm tap-target placement before shipping, especially any nested/adjacent structures.
alt: The human endocrine system, whole-body location map
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Hypothalamus
hotspots.detail: Links the nervous and endocrine systems; controls the pituitary gland via hormones.

hotspots.label: Pituitary gland
hotspots.detail: The **master gland** — hangs from the hypothalamus and controls most other endocrine glands.

hotspots.label: Thyroid gland
hotspots.detail: Butterfly-shaped gland in the neck; secretes thyroxine, regulating metabolic rate.

hotspots.label: Parathyroid glands
hotspots.detail: Small glands embedded in the thyroid; regulate blood calcium levels.

hotspots.label: Thymus
hotspots.detail: Produces hormones important for the development of the immune system, especially in childhood.

hotspots.label: Adrenal gland
hotspots.detail: Sits atop each kidney; secretes adrenaline and cortisol for stress response.

hotspots.label: Pancreas
hotspots.detail: Secretes insulin and glucagon, regulating blood glucose levels.

hotspots.label: Gonads
hotspots.detail: Testes or ovaries; secrete sex hormones controlling reproductive development.

generation_prompt: A clean modern educational anatomy illustration of the human body's endocrine system, shown as a front-facing outline of a human figure with internal glands visible through a subtle transparent silhouette. Show these structures clearly separated and individually distinguishable: hypothalamus, pituitary gland, pineal gland, thyroid gland, parathyroid glands, thymus, adrenal glands (one above each kidney), pancreas, and the gonads. Style: semi-realistic medical-textbook illustration, soft even studio lighting, smooth clean surfaces, a muted anatomical palette, faint pale outline for the body silhouette. Calm and flat: no glossy highlights, no neon, no glow, no 3D-render sheen. Composition: a single centered upright human figure, front view, generous even margins. Solid uniform deep-charcoal background, hex #121316, edge to edge. Absolutely no text, no labels, no numbers, no letters, no leader lines, no arrows, no legend, no watermark -- the illustration only. Portrait 3:4, high resolution.
